items.title: SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection
items.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates large language model (LLM) inference by using a small draft model to propose candidate tokens that a larger target model verifies. A critical hyperparameter in this process is the speculation length~$γ$, which determines how many tokens the draft model proposes per s...

items.title: Enhancing RL Generalizability in Robotics through SHAP Analysis of Algorithms and Hyperparameters
items.authors: Lingxiao Kong, Cong Yang, Oya Deniz Beyan et al.
items.abstract: Despite significant advances in Reinforcement Learning (RL), model performance remains highly sensitive to algorithm and hyperparameter configurations, while generalization gaps across environments complicate real-world deployment. items.title: FlexSQL: Flexible Exploration and Execution Make Better Text-to-SQL Agents
items.authors: Quang Hieu Pham, Yang He, Ping Nie et al.
items.abstract: Text-to-SQL over large analytical databases requires navigating complex schemas, resolving ambiguous queries, and grounding decisions in actual data. Most current systems follow a fixed pipeline where schema elements are retrieved once upfront and the database is only revisited for post-hoc repair, ...

items.title: AlbumFill: Album-Guided Reasoning and Retrieval for Personalized Image Completion
items.authors: Yu-Ju Tsai, Brian Price, Qing Liu et al.
items.abstract: Personalized image completion aims to restore occluded regions in personal photos while preserving identity and appearance. Existing methods either rely on generic inpainting models that often fail to maintain identity consistency, or assume that suitable reference images are explicitly provided. In...

items.title: Laplacian Frequency Interaction Network for Rural Thematic Road Extraction
items.abstract: Rural thematic road network construction aims to extract topological road structures from movement trajectory images of agricultural machinery. However, this task faces challenges where downsampling methods commonly used in existing studies tend to blur the sparse high-frequency road structures, and...

items.title: Unsupervised Machine Learning for Detecting Structural Anomalies in European Regional Statistics
items.abstract: Ensuring the coherence of regional socio-economic statistics is a central task for national statistical institutes. Traditional validation tools, such as range edits, ratio checks, or univariate outlier detection, are effective for identifying extreme values in individual series but are less suited ...

items.title: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment
items.authors: Haoquan Fang, Jiafei Duan, Donovan Clay et al.
items.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models aim to provide a single generalist controller for robots, but today's systems fall short on the criteria that matter for real-world deployment. Frontier models are closed, open-weight alternatives are tied to expensive hardware, reasoning-augmented policies pay pr...
